title: Delicate Argentinian Sweet Potato Empanadas
description: Yeasty, crisp empanadas filled with sweet potato, fermented kimchi and aromatic herbs, perfect for a vegetarian weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a bowl, combine flour, sugar, salt, and yeast.
Add warm water and olive oil; mix until dough forms.
Knead dough on a floured surface for 8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place for 45 minutes until doubled.
While dough rises, sauté onion and garlic in olive oil until translucent.
Add diced sweet potato and kimchi to the sautéed onion and garlic, cook for 5 minutes, season with oregano, salt, and pepper.
Roll dough into 15cm circles on a floured surface.
Spoon filling onto the center of each circle, fold dough into a triangle, and seal edges with a fork.
Brush empanadas with beaten egg or plant milk and bake at 200 degrees Celsius for 15 minutes until golden.
Let cool for 5 minutes, then serve warm with a side of salsa.
